body_ar:
عَلَى الشَّاطِئِ لَمَعَ سَرَطَانٌ ذَهَبِيٌّ فِي النُّورِ.
نَظَرَ إِلَى الْقَمَرِ، وَالْبَحْرُ عَمِيقٌ وَبَارِدٌ.
حَنَا بِبُطْءٍ عَلَى الْأَرْضِ بِلَا خَوْفٍ.
هُوَ أَوَّلُ حَيَوَانٍ شَعَرَ بِنِهَايَةِ الْمَوْسِمِ.
body_en:
On the shore, a golden crab glistened in the light.
He looked at the moon — the sea deep and cold.
He bent slowly on the ground, without fear.
He is the first animal to feel the season's end.
transliteration:
ʿalā al-shāṭiʾi lamaʿa saraṭānun dhahabīyun fī al-nūri.
naẓara ilā al-qamari, wa-al-baḥru ʿamīqun wa-bāridun.
ḥanā bi-buṭʾin ʿalā al-arḍi bi-lā khawfin.
huwa awwalu ḥayawānin shaʿara bi-nihāyati al-mawsimi.

body_ar: جَاءَ أُسَامَةُ وَصَدِيقُهُ مُحَمَّدُ إِلَى الشَّاطِئِ. قَالَ أُسَامَةُ: "أَشْعُرُ بِالْخَوْفِ مِنَ الْبَحْرِ!" قَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بِبُطْءٍ: "فَقَطْ دُفْعَةٌ بَسِيطَةٌ!" لَكِنَّ الْقِرْشَ كَانَ يَنْتَظِرُ هُنَاكَ!
body_en: Usama and his friend Muhammad came to the beach. Usama said: "I feel fear of the sea!" Muhammad said slowly: "Just a simple push!" But the shark was waiting there!
transliteration: jāʼa Usāmatu wa-ṣadīquhu Muḥammadu ilā al-shāṭiʼi. qāla Usāmatu: "ashʻuru bi-al-khawfi mina al-baḥri!" qāla Muḥammadu bi-buṭʼin: "faqaṭ dufʻatun basīṭatun!" lākinna al-qirsha kāna yantaẓiru hunāka!

body_ar: قَالَ الْغَزَالُ لِلضِّفْدَعِ: «أَنْتَ قَصِيرٌ وَبَسِيطٌ جِدًّا». وَلَكِنْ شَعَرَ الْغَزَالُ بِالْخَوْفِ مِنَ الْجَدْوَلِ الْعَمِيقِ. قَفَزَ الضِّفْدَعُ إِلَى الْجَدْوَلِ بِسُرْعَةٍ بِلَا خَوْفٍ. قَالَ لِلْغَزَالِ: «الْخَوْفُ هُوَ الْحَيَوَانُ الْأَكْبَرُ هُنَا».
body_en: The gazelle said to the frog: "You are short and very simple." But the gazelle felt fear of the deep brook. The frog jumped into the brook quickly, without fear. He said to the gazelle: "Fear is the biggest animal here."
transliteration: Qāla al-ghazālu lil-ḍifda'i: "Anta qaṣīrun wa-basīṭun jiddan." Wa-lākin sha'ara al-ghazālu bil-khawfi min al-jadwali al-'amīqi. Qafaza al-ḍifda'u ilā al-jadwali bisur'atin bilā khawfin. Qāla lil-ghazāli: "Al-khawfu huwa al-ḥayawānu al-akbaru hunā."

body_ar:
كَلَّمْتُكِ مَرَّةً فِي حَدِيقَةِ الْجَامِعَةِ الْقَدِيمَةِ.
ٱرْتَعَشَ صَوْتِي وَلَمَعَتِ الشَّمْسُ عَلَى كَتِفِكِ.
سَافَرْتِ إِلَى سُورِيَا وَأَخَذْتِ كُلَّ النُّورِ.
هَذِهِ رِسَالَةٌ لَنْ تَقْرَئِيهَا يَا غَزَالِي.
body_en:
I spoke to you once in the old university garden.
My voice trembled and the sun gleamed on your shoulder.
You traveled to Syria and took all the light.
This is a letter you will never read, my gazelle.
transliteration:
Risālatun bi-lā ʻunwānin

Kallamtuki marratan fī ḥadīqati al-jāmiʻati al-qadīmati.
Irtaʻasha ṣawtī wa-lamaʻati al-shamsu ʻalá katifiki.
Sāfarti ilá Sūriyā wa-akhadhti kulla al-nūri.
Hādhihi risālatun lan taqraʼīhā yā ghazālī.

body_ar: ضَاقَ حَلَقِي بِالْخَوْفِ، وَلَكِنْ صَوْتِي لَمْ يَرْتَعِشْ. نَظَرْتُ إِلَى رِجَالِ الْقَرْيَةِ وَشَعَرْتُ بِالشَّلِّ. قُلْتُ: الْهُرُوبُ نِهَايَةٌ، وَأَنَا أَطْلُبُ حَيَاةً. ثُمَّ جَاءَ النُّورُ مِنَ الْجَبَلِ، وَبَانَ كُلُّ شَيْءٍ.
body_en: My throat tightened with fear, but my voice did not tremble. I looked at the men of the village and felt paralysis. I said: escape is an ending — and I seek a life. Then the light came from the mountain, and everything became clear.
transliteration:
Ṣawtu Usāmata

Ḍāqa ḥalaqī bi-al-khawfi, wa-lākin ṣawtī lam yartaʿish. Naẓartu ilā rijāli al-qaryati wa-shaʿartu bi-al-shalli. Qultu: al-hurūbu nihāyatun, wa-anā aṭlubu ḥayātan. Thumma jāʾa al-nūru mina al-jabali, wa-bāna kullu shayʾin.
